oai_dc:dc.dc:title: A Young Aviculturist
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Del Rio, Cortez
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: This article originally appeared in AFA Watchbird Volume 51 Number 3 (July 2024). The text below contains excerpts from the original article. For photographs, figures, advertisements, and the complete formatted version as originally published, please use the PDF link associated with this article. Age: 10 years old. Cortez is also featured on page 16 holding a male Red Golden Pheasant. Hometown: Wilder, Idaho, USA. School: HomeEd 360 Charter Homeschool. Hobbies: Legos, dirt bikes, firearms, camping, aviculture, video games. Species Focus: Scientific Name: Chrysolophus pictus. Common Name: Golden Pheasant. Native Range: Western to Central China. Wild Habitat: Mountain Forests. Wild Status: Least Concern (IUCN). Aviculture Status: Common. Mutations: Dark-throated, peach/splash, Ghigi's Yellow. Egg Clutch: 6-20 eggs. Incubation Period: 22-23 days. Size/Weight: Males 1-2 lbs, 32-39 inches long; females 1-1.5 lbs, 24-28 inches long. Wild Diet: omnivorous - grains, seeds, fallen fruit, berries, grasses, and insects. Q: When do you remember the first time you saw Golden Pheasants? A: I saw them at John Redmon's Gamebird Farm when I was 5 years old. Q: Why does our family keep Golden Pheasants? A: It is an ornamental bird and they are very beautiful to look at. Q: What is your favorite thing about Golden Pheasants? A: My favorite is the Red Golden Pheasant. I like to watch them dance around. Q: What do we feed the adult Golden Pheasants? A: We feed a local gamebird grain mix plus fruit and berries. Q: What type of cage do we keep the Golden Pheasants in? A: A walk-in aviary that is 8' wide by 24' long by 8' tall. Click the PDF link associated with this article to view the complete article as originally published in AFA Watchbird.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: AFA Watchbird; Vol. 51 No. 3 (2024): AFA Watchbird July 2024
